rules_fired.because: Liquids with freeze points at or above 15°C (DMSO ~19°C, glycerol ~18°C) can solidify partially or fully in a 4°C cold room or on ice. Pipetting a partially-solidified liquid causes systematic volume errors. The physical_state guard prevents the rule from misfiring on solids whose melting point happens to be reported in freeze_point_C (e.g. NaOH pellets, PFA powder).
rules_fired.cite: Sigma-Aldrich DMSO Product Information Sheet 276855 (freeze point 18.45°C). Perry's Chemical Engineers' Handbook, 8th ed. (glycerol freeze point 17.8°C).

rules_fired.because: Skin-penetrant reagents cross intact skin and can carry dissolved solutes transdermally. DMSO is the canonical example — contact with a DMSO drug stock is a real toxicity exposure route even through nitrile gloves with extended contact. 2-mercaptoethanol and acrylamide are similarly absorbed through skin. Nitrile gloves (not latex) are required; change gloves immediately after any spill; never work with these without gloves.
rules_fired.cite: NIOSH skin absorption hazard notes. Braue EH et al. (1999) Occup Environ Med: percutaneous absorption review.
